triageFlags.title: Excessive Vehicle Mileage
triageFlags.description: The vehicle's mileage exceeds the maximum allowed at the start of the agreement.
triageFlags.recommendation: Consider requesting a justification or alternative vehicle with lower mileage.
triageFlags.reasoning: Policy states a maximum mileage of 100,000 miles at the start of the agreement. This vehicle's mileage exceeds this limit, potentially impacting its residual value.

triageFlags.title: High Loan-to-Value Ratio
triageFlags.description: The Loan-to-Value ratio exceeds the maximum allowed for the applicant's credit tier.
triageFlags.recommendation: Request a higher deposit or adjust the loan amount to comply with the LTV policy.
triageFlags.reasoning: For a Tier B credit score, the maximum LTV is 85%. The current LTV of 91.11% exceeds this limit, increasing the financial risk.

content.reasoning: The identified issues, while not critical, suggest a need for further scrutiny to ensure compliance with policy and mitigate potential risks.
